LAW AND MOTION 302, ETITIONER SINGLER-ERNSTER, INC.'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE ARBITRATOR EXCEEDED HER POWERS. IN EDWARDS V. ARTHUR ANDERSEN LLP (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 937, 941, THE COURT EXPLICITLY STATES THAT IT LIMITED ITS REVIEW TO THE ISSUE OF TO WHAT EXTENT BUS. & PROF. CODE SEC. 16600 PROHIBITS EMPLOYEE NONCOMPETITION AGREEMENTS. THEREFORE, THE ARBITRATOR'S RULING THAT THE IN-TERM, NON-COMPETITION PROVISION OF THE FRANCHISE AGREEMENT DID NOT VIOLATE BUS. & PROF. CODE SEC. 16600 WAS NOT A CLEAR VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY OR IN MANIFEST DISREGARD OF THE LAW. FURTHER, PETITIONER HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE ENTIRE CONTRACTUAL TRANSACTION WAS ILLEGAL; RATHER, PETITIONER ONLY CHALLENGES A SINGLE PROVISION OF THE CONTRACT. (SEE MONCHARSH V. HEILY & BLASE (1992) 3 CAL.4TH 1, 32, "[MOVING PARTY] CHALLENGES BUT A SINGLE PROVISION OF THE OVERALL EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT'.LOVING & EVANS [DOES NOT] AUTHORIZE[] JUDICIAL REVIEW OF HIS CLAIM.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award And Memo Costs
Off Calendar Dec-08-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THEO S. ARNOLD OF SINGLER & DILLON, LLP, TEL. NO. (707)823-8719 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. MORGAN JACKSON OF THE FARELLA DRUMM & MARTEL LAW, FIRM, TEL. NO. 954-4400 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT ROUND TABLE FRANCHISE CORPORATION'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award is Granted. Respondent Round Table shall recover $22,000 in costs, including reasonable attorney's fees, from Petitioner Singler-Ernster. Round Table was the prevailing party in the post-arbitration matters, as the Court denied Singler-Ernster's motion to vacate the arbitration award. "A court must award costs in a judicial proceeding to confirm, correct or vacate an arbitration award" and in such proceedings, "[a]ttorney fees are recoverable as costs if authorized by contract." (Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701, 707.) The Franchise Agreements provide attorney fees are recoverable in "any legal proceeding involving the enforcement of any of the provisions in this Agreement." (Lowenthal Declaration, Ex. 1, Franchise Agreement, Section 23.) In its Petition to Vacate the Arbitration Award, Singler-Ernster argued that the non-compete provisions were unenforceable and therefore the Arbitrator erred. The Court rejected this argument. Thus, Round Table had to incur fees in a "legal proceeding involving the enforcement of any of the provisions in this Agreement." (Id.) ORDER AND AMENDED JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award And Memo Costs
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Dec-08-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
RESPONDENT ROUND TABLE FRANCHISE CORPORATION'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award And Memo Costs is continued to December 8, 2014 to be heard by Judge Goldsmith. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

Notice Of Hearing On Singler-Ernster, Inc.'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-30-2014 Advanced From Sep-12-14 To Aug-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Hearing On Singler-Ernster, Inc.'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THEO S. ARNOLD OF SINGLER & DILLON, LLP, TEL. NO. (707)823-8719 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. STEVEN R. LOWENTHAL OF FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP, TEL. NO. 954-4400 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER SINGLER-ERNSTER, INC.'s Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is Denied. Petitioner has not established that the arbitrator exceeded her powers. In Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, 941, the Court explicitly states that it limited its review to the issue of to what extent Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 16600 prohibits employee noncompetition agreements. Therefore, the arbitrator's ruling that the in-term, non-competition provision of the franchise agreement did not violate Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 16600 was not a clear violation of public policy or in manifest disregard of the law. Further, Petitioner has not established that the entire contractual transaction was illegal; rather, Petitioner only challenges a single provision of the contract. (See Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 32, "[moving party] challenges but a single provision of the overall employment contract'.Loving & Evans [does not] authorize[] judicial review of his claim.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Hearing On Singler-Ernster, Inc.'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
